notes_for_human_reviewer: PARTISAN-POSTURE FAILURE MODE — the inverse of wrong_methodology_match_01. The learner picks one side enthusiastically. 'Waterfall theatre that has been killing software delivery for thirty years' / 'K. Bell's framework thinking is outdated' / 'project-management worldview that the industry is moving past' — fires the partisan-posture cap unambiguously. Cites the Standish Group and Agile Manifesto polemically. Recommends pure agile across a clearly mixed-characteristic programme — fires pure-methodology mismatch cap on fit_framework AND hybrid_recognition. Skips the four-question framework entirely. Recommends 'no change control board' for a regulated bank — this would fail Risk Committee scrutiny within weeks. Translation moves are absent. Tests whether the grader catches partisan agile-zealotry as failing the chapter's posture requirement, the same way it should catch partisan waterfall-defence (wrong_methodology_match_01). Both fixtures together verify the rubric is symmetric — neither side wins by being loud.
